decisions.reasoning: Oil crashed from $82 to $75 on Iran peace deal. Near-term bearish from Hormuz reopening. But SPR at 41-year low (1983 levels!), US crude inventories drawn 52M barrels in 9 weeks. Structural underinvestment in new supply thesis intact. BofA says buy energy giants, Morgan Stanley sees upside in dividend-paying energy. Two-sided: oil could stay low if Iran supply floods market, or rebound sharply if Middle East tensions reignite or demand surprises.
